world_date: Month of the Ashen Dreaming, Year 1 of the Hollow Accord, Day 32
major_event: Day thirty-two: The Varek-Ironmaul audience occurs in Lostfeld's hall — Varek speaks of the entity as a sovereign, not a prisoner. Both of Ashveil's documents arrive at Stonebreak. The Seer delivers his interpretation of the Tidefall otters to Admiral Ver Meer: the God watches through water. Caeris Thorne makes first contact with Elian Adkison before dawn.

primary_event.name: The Varek Audience — Druid Confronts Thane in the Hall of Seals
primary_event.summary: At noon on Day 32, Grand Druid Varek was brought before Thane Ironmaul in Lostfeld's hall. He entered without ceremony, wearing the clean clothes sent by Ironmaul, and addressed the Thane as one sovereign addresses another. He stated that the entity beneath the floor had chosen him, that its choosing was not a matter of dwarven law, and that no seal could contain what had already spoken. The Runewardens scribes recorded everything. Korin Ironmaul's hand moved to his weapon and stopped. The clan elders in the room formed incompatible judgments by the time Varek finished speaking.

supporting_events.name: Ashveil's Documents Arrive at Stonebreak — Council Reads Both Letters
supporting_events.summary: Both sealed transcript and Ashveil's second letter arrived at Stonebreak by midmorning on Day 32. The Senior Council convened in full session by midday and read them consecutively. The transcript confirms a binding candidate is communicating through the entity. The second letter urges institutional intervention before the candidate's detention becomes precedent. The Council has begun deliberating but has not yet issued a response.

supporting_events.name: Seer Delivers Formal Interpretation — The God Watches Through Water
supporting_events.summary: The Seer met with Admiral Ver Meer in the Admiral's chambers on Day 32 morning, before the audience crowds gathered at the harbor. His formal interpretation: the otters were not a message to Tidefall specifically — they were a public sign made in the most visible place available, an announcement of presence. He said the God watches through moving water and had chosen the harbor because it was where the most eyes would see. The Admiral listened, asked no questions, and dismissed the Seer with formal courtesy.

supporting_events.name: Caeris Thorne — First Contact with Elian Adkison Before Dawn
supporting_events.summary: Caeris Thorne sent a sealed note to Elian Adkison's quarters before dawn on Day 32 — not through the usual house channels but delivered by her personal attendant with instructions to wait for a verbal acknowledgment only. The note contained a single question: whether Elian had seen the Tidefall harbor report. It was a test of his curiosity and his discretion simultaneously. Elian confirmed receipt by word only. Caeris has not revealed her purpose. The door is now open.

active_events.name: Varek-Ironmaul Audience — Peak Confrontation, Hall of Seals
active_events.summary: The audience occurred as scheduled. Varek spoke with the authority of someone who believes his appointment is cosmological rather than institutional. Ironmaul responded with formal dwarven law and the weight of his own hall behind him. The scribes recorded both positions. No violence. No resolution. The confrontation has clarified that these two men cannot both be right, and both believe they are.
active_events.consequences: Ironmaul must now respond to Varek's claim institutionally. Dismissing him outright risks making a martyr; granting legitimacy risks ceding clan authority over their own sealed mines. The Monastery's response, arriving separately, will either align with Varek and escalate to diplomatic crisis, or diverge and undercut him. Either outcome reshapes the Lostfeld situation.

active_events.name: Stonebreak Senior Council — Full Deliberation Underway
active_events.summary: The Council read both documents in full session on Day 32. The transcript is formal evidence; Ashveil's letter is an institutional appeal. The Council is divided: the cautious faction argues that Varek entered Lostfeld voluntarily and dwarven hospitality law is not their jurisdiction; the interventionist faction argues that a binding candidate held without Monastery sanction is a violation of their sacred mandate. A formal response will be drafted by Day 33 at the earliest.
active_events.consequences: The Council's response will determine whether this becomes a diplomatic confrontation between the Monastery and the Lostfeld Dwarves, or whether Varek is left to negotiate alone. Ironmaul does not yet know the Council is reading the documents — the timing of his audience with Varek may precede the Council's formal position.

active_events.name: Caeris Thorne — Religious Foundation Initializing
active_events.summary: First contact made before dawn on Day 32. The note was a probe — Caeris is assessing whether Elian can be trusted with something larger than treasury work. His verbal-only acknowledgment and his discretion in not mentioning the note to other house members suggests he understands that he is being tested. Caeris has not yet revealed the nature of what she is building. The next contact will be more direct.
active_events.consequences: If Elian passes the next test, Caeris has her instrument. She must begin the religion before she moves on the throne — which means she needs at least one trusted intermediary who can receive the early teachings without immediately politicizing them. Elian is young enough, curious enough, and low-visibility enough to serve that function. The risk is that his moderate loyalty means he may share what he learns if he fears consequences.

active_events.name: Shadow Court — Lythara's Debrief Arrives Tomorrow, Arkenor Collision Imminent
active_events.summary: The debrief courier left Tidefall's outer harbor on Day 31. It will reach Lythara on Day 33. She has spent Day 32 continuing the Ver Meer shipyard insertion, unaware that the debrief she is about to receive will confirm the Seer's presence in Tidefall and the otter sign — and that she still does not know about the Arkenor agreement. The collision is one day away.
active_events.consequences: When Lythara reads the debrief, she will learn: the Seer is in Tidefall, forty otters appeared publicly, and the harbor population has begun interpreting it spiritually. She will have to decide how the God's public sign affects her existing operations. Meanwhile the Arkenor situation will surface eventually — and when it does, Lythara's reaction to a unilateral decision made beneath her will be severe.

active_events.name: Tidefall Otter Interpretation — Harbor Belief Currents Forming
active_events.summary: The Seer's formal interpretation — the God watches through moving water — has now spread from the Admiral's chambers into the harbor district through the servants present at the delivery and through Isolde Ver Meer, who was taking notes during the session. By afternoon of Day 32, the fishermen who witnessed the otters are repeating the phrase 'watches through water' to each other. Isolde's port annals search found no prior otter appearance on record.
active_events.consequences: The interpretation is becoming doctrine before it is even argued. The phrase is memorable and requires no theological sophistication to repeat. If it spreads to other ports, the otter event becomes the founding moment of a belief current, not merely an anomaly. The Admiral's surveillance of the harbor mood confirms that fishermen are treating the event as sacred rather than strange.

active_events.name: Farrock Garrison Sickness — Day Two, Cause Still Unknown
active_events.summary: The garrison sickness continues on Day 32. No new soldiers have collapsed but the forty quarantined remain ill. The reserve cistern water tests clean. The commander sent riders to both DK and Twin Cities on Day 31 — no reply yet, given travel time. The Gloomspire keeper who acquired the transcriptions from the tinker is now out of Farrock and beyond the garrison lockdown. DK's factor is still waiting inside the lockdown.
active_events.consequences: The cipher fragment remains in the commander's personal strongbox. Gloomspire has the transcriptions. DK's factor cannot acquire the primary fragment until the garrison reopens. If the sickness has a supernatural cause, Farrock's commander will seek the Monastery. If it has a natural cause, DK will be first to offer medical aid and may leverage that for access.

active_events.name: Morgath's Ridge Hold — Stonejaw Arrives Tomorrow, Day Three of Four
active_events.summary: Stonejaw's column is one day from the Groth border ridge. Morgath's captains are in their third day of a four-day hold. Morgath still does not know Stonejaw is riding. Glenhaven's second observer group is following Stonejaw at distance. The confrontation will occur on Day 33 — Stonejaw arrives while Morgath's captains are still in position with one day remaining on their order.
active_events.consequences: The confrontation will be direct. Stonejaw will arrive to find the captains in position and Morgath's authority asserted on the ridge. He carries the caravan master's testimony. How Morgath's captains respond to the First Elder's arrival — whether they hold, stand aside, or send immediate word to Morgath — will determine whether this becomes a diplomatic resolution or the first crack in inter-orc unity.

active_events.name: Rowen's Fleet — Pre-Language Carvings Studied, Sinking Island Course Possible
active_events.summary: Day 32 at the arch formation. Briggs spent the morning comparing the pre-language carvings against his navigational charts and against the stone sample composition. He believes the arches were built when the sea level was lower — which means they predate the current coastal geography. Rowen has authorized a modest ration draw for the day and is planning a course bearing from the arch formation's alignment.
active_events.consequences: If the arch alignment functions as a navigational marker as Briggs suspects, Rowen may have a bearing toward the sinking island without needing additional landmarks. His crew's name for the formation — the Gate — has become standard usage. Stores are holding but not unlimited. He has perhaps three more days at this location before the ration calculus forces a decision.

faction_actions.action: Caeris Thorne sent the sealed note to Elian Adkison before dawn. She spent the morning hours preparing a second communication — not yet sent — that will invite Elian to a private meeting in the treasury annex under the pretext of a supply discrepancy audit. Meanwhile Halveth was dispatched with the DK premium authorization to the Farrock factor, not yet knowing the garrison was still under lockdown. King Roderic received the Farrock sickness report via the commander's courier and authorized emergency grain and medicine to be prepared for dispatch — standard ruler response, no supernatural interpretation.
faction_actions.reason: Caeris is moving with deliberate precision. The Elian channel is her lowest-risk first move. Roderic's response to Farrock is competent but secular — he sees a medical crisis, not an omen. The two Thornian responses to the same morning are becoming a study in different worldviews occupying the same palace.
faction_actions.target: Elian Adkison — first contact made, second contact being prepared; Farrock — Roderic authorizes emergency medical aid; Halveth — dispatched with cipher fragment authorization

faction_actions.action: Ironmaul presided over the audience as planned. He let Varek speak fully without interruption — a deliberate display of dwarven composure. When Varek finished, Ironmaul responded with three sentences: Lostfeld's seals are Clan Ironmaul's sovereign responsibility. Varek entered Lostfeld as a guest of the Monastery and remains a guest. No entity beneath dwarven stone confers authority over dwarven law. He then adjourned the session and ordered Varek returned to his quarters with both doors unlocked — emphasizing that Varek was a guest, not a prisoner. He privately ordered Torvek Runewarden's mine shaft report to be delivered to him before nightfall.
faction_actions.reason: Ironmaul's three sentences are a masterwork of constrained response. He does not deny the entity spoke. He does not deny Varek's experience. He simply asserts that none of it overrides clan sovereignty. The unlocked door is a calculated civility — it prevents martyrdom while maintaining authority. He wants Torvek's report before he decides anything further.
faction_actions.target: Varek — audience concluded, returned to unlocked quarters; Torvek — mine report expected by nightfall; clan elders — instructed to keep session contents confidential until Ironmaul issues formal statement

faction_actions.action: The Senior Council read both documents in full session. By midday they had separated into two clear camps: the Jurisdiction Faction, led by Senior Chair Bren Vaultwood, who argues that Varek entered Lostfeld voluntarily and the Monastery cannot compel dwarven release; and the Mandate Faction, led by Elder Thornmoss, who argues that any binding candidate held without Monastery sanction violates the sacred compact regardless of political borders. Ashveil received no reply yet — the Council is drafting. She dispatched her third personal note to Senior Chair Vaultwood regardless, scheduled for Day 34 delivery if no response by then.
faction_actions.reason: The Council's split is ideologically genuine and politically consequential. If the Jurisdiction Faction prevails, Varek is on his own and Ironmaul wins by default. If the Mandate Faction prevails, the Monastery will formally demand Varek's release — which Ironmaul will refuse — and the confrontation escalates to an institutional crisis.
faction_actions.target: Senior Chair Vaultwood — deliberation leader, Jurisdiction Faction; Elder Thornmoss — Mandate Faction; Ashveil — third note prepared, awaiting Day 34 delivery trigger; Varek — Council aware of audience, awaiting outcome

faction_actions.action: Lythara advanced the Ver Meer shipyard insertion on Day 32: the wood-merchant contact met with Maeric Ver Meer directly in the shipyard offices under the commercial pretext and established a preliminary business relationship. No intelligence was exchanged yet — this was a trust-building contact. Lythara's debrief courier from Tidefall is now one day away. She remains unaware of the otter event's interpretation by the Seer and unaware of the Arkenor agreement. She also ordered Ryss Nightborn to quietly assess what the Gloomspire Syndicate's Farrock keeper might know about the garrison sickness.
faction_actions.reason: Lythara operates on two timelines simultaneously. The insertion advances. The debrief approaches. She is gathering intelligence on the Farrock situation through Ryss as a secondary inquiry — the garrison sickness is geopolitically interesting to her regardless of cause.
faction_actions.target: Maeric Ver Meer — direct contact established under commercial pretext; Ryss Nightborn — Gloomspire Farrock inquiry ordered; debrief courier — one day out; Arkenor agreement — still unknown to Lythara

faction_actions.action: Elowen received confirmation from her border corridor observers that Stonejaw's column was on schedule and would reach the Groth ridge by midday on Day 33. She sent a second courtesy message to Frostvale — this one more specific: Glenhaven's observers would be present at the confrontation as formal witnesses. She also convened a quiet consultation with Taelis Silverleaf about what Glenhaven's position should be if Morgath refuses to withdraw and Stonejaw calls for a council response. They discussed no military options.
faction_actions.reason: Elowen is positioning Glenhaven as the witness-power in this confrontation — the faction whose presence makes the outcome formally recorded. Consulting Taelis about a council response is preparation without commitment. She will not intervene militarily under any foreseeable scenario.
faction_actions.target: Frostvale — second message sent, observer presence formalized; Taelis Silverleaf — council response options discussed; Stonejaw — on schedule, Glenhaven tracking; Morgath — still unaware of Glenhaven witness positioning

faction_actions.action: Stonejaw's column made good time on Day 32. His advance rider returned with the Glenhaven observers' exact position — they were following at approximately two hours' distance, non-threatening. Stonejaw composed a brief declaration during the evening camp: when he arrives at the ridge, he will present the caravan master's testimony directly to Morgath's senior captain and demand a withdrawal acknowledgment within the hour. He is treating this as a jurisdiction assertion, not a negotiation.
faction_actions.reason: Stonejaw's hour-demand is aggressive by elder-council standards — it reflects how seriously he views Morgath's unilateral action near Frostvale. He is not asking. He is asserting that Gilgeth's elder council has authority to demand compliance on inter-orc territorial matters. This will force Morgath's captains to either comply without Morgath's direct order or send for Morgath — which will take longer than an hour.
faction_actions.target: Advance rider — Glenhaven position confirmed; Morgath's senior captain — jurisdiction demand being prepared; testimony — to be presented upon arrival Day 33; Glenhaven observers — acknowledged, treated as witnesses

faction_actions.action: Morgath spent Day 32 in his highland camp, still interpreting Frostvale's silence as capitulation. His captains on the ridge sent their regular report: no Frostvale sallies, no Gilgeth movement visible. Morgath began composing terms he intended to send to Frostvale's gate captain on Day 33 — a demand for seasonal passage rights through the ridge corridor in exchange for withdrawing his hold. He does not know Stonejaw is arriving tomorrow. He does not know Glenhaven observers are present.
faction_actions.reason: Morgath's aggressive-paranoid intelligence is producing a classic overreach: he is escalating toward formal demands precisely as his leverage is about to evaporate. His hold order expires on Day 34. He is trying to extract a concession before he has to withdraw anyway. He will be confronted by Stonejaw before his demand can be delivered.
faction_actions.target: Frostvale — terms being composed for Day 33 delivery; captains on ridge — holding, Day 3 of 4; Stonejaw — completely unknown; Glenhaven — completely unknown

faction_actions.action: The Farrock keeper reached the Gloomspire waystation south of Farrock by midday on Day 32 with the transcriptions secured. He dispatched a coded message to syndicate council: acquisition successful, transcriptions in hand, no complications, DK factor and Twin Cities liaison both stalled by garrison lockdown. The council received confirmation by evening and issued a follow-up order: the transcriptions are to be stored at the Gloomspire mountain archive under cipher seal, accessible only by council authorization.
faction_actions.reason: The syndicate is treating the cipher fragment transcriptions as a long-term asset rather than an immediate commodity. They will not sell what they cannot yet understand. The archive seal means no faction can purchase or steal them without Gloomspire knowledge.
faction_actions.target: Transcriptions — secured, dispatched to mountain archive; DK factor — stalled, syndicate not concerned; council — satisfied with acquisition outcome; cipher fragment primary — still in Farrock commander's strongbox

faction_actions.action: After the Seer's formal interpretation, Admiral Ver Meer ordered Isolde Ver Meer to compile a written summary of the Seer's statement for the admiralty record. He also ordered harbor surveillance continued for three more days and directed Cassian Adkison to draft a confidential report on the harbor population's mood shift — specifically whether the 'watches through water' interpretation was spreading or fading. The Admiral then returned to his regular schedule: fleet supply orders, Dreadwind patrol reporting, trade lane assessment.
faction_actions.reason: Ver Meer is methodical. He has received an interpretation. He has documented it. He will now watch whether it propagates or dissipates. If it propagates, it becomes a political variable. He does not act on spiritual matters — he acts on political consequences of spiritual matters. His Dreadwind patrol reporting reminds him that the sea conflict with Dreadwind is the operational priority.
faction_actions.target: Isolde — Seer statement compiled for admiralty record; Cassian Adkison — harbor mood report drafted; surveillance — three more days authorized; Dreadwind patrols — routine reports received

faction_actions.action: DK's factor at Farrock sent Day 32 report: garrison still under partial lockdown, quarantine holding at forty soldiers, no new collapses, no medical relief yet from Twin Cities or DK. The Council reviewed and authorized a medical team dispatch — three healers and emergency herb supplies, departing Day 32 afternoon, arriving Farrock Day 34. They also received the Gloomspire inquiry response: the waystation keeper had departed Farrock before the lockdown and was unavailable for comment. DK noted the timing suspiciously.
faction_actions.reason: DK's medical dispatch is opportunistic generosity — arriving as relief positions them for access to the commander and the cipher fragment. The Gloomspire keeper's convenient departure timing is a red flag to DK's analytically suspicious Council. They now suspect the syndicate may have acquired something from Farrock before the lockdown.
faction_actions.target: Medical team — three healers dispatched, arriving Day 34; Farrock factor — hold and monitor authorization continued; Gloomspire — suspected of prior acquisition; cipher fragment — suspected compromised but unconfirmed

faction_actions.action: The tinker, now safely out of Farrock, returned to the Rock Plains border market. Skrix Cogtooth received a routine market report that included a note: a human tinker had sold cipher-related papers to a glass merchant at Farrock before the garrison lockdown. Skrix does not know who the glass merchant was. He flagged the report and passed it to Vrax Cogtooth for investigation. Skrix also issued a small increase to the Rock Plains border patrol — the Groth ridge tension makes him cautious about movement near the Groth-Vilefin border.
faction_actions.reason: Skrix is connecting sparse dots. The cipher papers note is imprecise but he recognizes that cipher-related materials near Farrock have strategic value. His Groth border patrol increase reflects a practical awareness that Morgath's aggressive posture could spill toward Vilefin territory if unchecked.
faction_actions.target: Vrax Cogtooth — Farrock cipher report investigation ordered; border patrol — increased near Groth-Vilefin line; tinker — safe, market-returned, no further contact with events

faction_actions.action: Rowen spent Day 32 with Briggs analyzing the arch carvings and the stone sample. By evening Briggs presented a working hypothesis: the arches were constructed as navigational gates, and their alignment when corrected for magnetic deviation points toward a bearing in the southwest quadrant of the open sea — consistent with a location that would have been above water when sea levels were lower. Rowen ordered the fleet to prepare to move on Day 33 morning toward that bearing. Full ration for the transit day authorized.
faction_actions.reason: Rowen's intelligence is high enough to recognize that Briggs's hypothesis is speculative but directionally sound. He is not gambling the fleet on an uncertain landmark — he is following a navigational inference from physical evidence. His crew is invested in the mission through the 'gate' naming. Moving on Day 33 commits him to a bearing that could take several days to resolve.
faction_actions.target: Briggs — southwest bearing calculated; fleet — departure preparation ordered for Day 33 morning; stores — one more full ration day authorized; sinking island bearing — tentative southwest quadrant

recent_events.text: The Varek-Ironmaul audience occurred at noon. Varek spoke without deference, claiming the entity's choosing as cosmological authority. Ironmaul responded with three formal sentences of dwarven law and adjourned the session. Varek was returned to unlocked quarters. The clan elders in the room hold conflicting assessments. No resolution. The confrontation has clarified the incompatibility of both men's positions.

Both of Ashveil's documents arrived at Stonebreak by midmorning. The Council read them in full session. They have split into two factions: the Jurisdiction camp argues Varek entered voluntarily and the Monastery cannot compel dwarven release; the Mandate camp argues a binding candidate held without sanction violates sacred compact. A formal response is being drafted. No word sent to Ironmaul or Varek yet.

recent_events.region: Tidefall — Admiral's Chambers and Harbor District
recent_events.text: The Seer met with Admiral Ver Meer before the morning crowds. His interpretation: the God watches through moving water, and the otters appeared in Tidefall because it was the most visible harbor. The phrase 'watches through water' has already spread through the harbor district by afternoon. Isolde confirmed no prior otter appearance in port annals. The Admiral documented the interpretation formally and returned to fleet business.

Caeris Thorne sent a sealed note to Elian Adkison before dawn — a test of his curiosity and discretion. He acknowledged verbally only and told no one. Caeris has identified him as a viable first instrument for her religious work. A second contact, framed as a treasury audit meeting, is being prepared. She still has not revealed her purpose.

Stonejaw's column camped one day from the Groth border ridge on Day 32 evening. He has composed an hour-deadline demand to be delivered to Morgath's senior captain upon arrival. Morgath, still unaware, spent Day 32 composing passage terms he intends to send to Frostvale tomorrow — arriving just as Stonejaw does. The confrontation window is now hours away.

active_tensions.factions: Lostfeld — Post-Audience, Two Irreconcilable Claims, Monastery Response Incoming
active_tensions.description: Varek and Ironmaul have stated their incompatible positions formally, on the record, in front of witnesses. Neither can retreat without losing authority. The Monastery is drafting a response that will either support Varek institutionally or leave him isolated. Ironmaul has not yet seen the Monastery's documents — he does not know the Council is deliberating as he speaks.

active_tensions.factions: Gilgeth-Groth — Confrontation Hours Away, Morgath Composing Demands While Stonejaw Rides
active_tensions.description: Stonejaw arrives at the ridge on Day 33 with an hour-deadline demand while Morgath's captains are still in position. Morgath is simultaneously composing passage terms he intends to send to Frostvale — which will never be delivered because Stonejaw will arrive first. Two processes are colliding at the same point in time.

active_tensions.factions: Tidefall — Seer's Interpretation Spreading, 'Watches Through Water' Becoming First Doctrine
active_tensions.description: The phrase from the Seer's formal interpretation has left the Admiral's chambers and entered the harbor population's daily speech. Isolde is tracking its spread. The God's public sign has now received an authoritative interpretation from the world's most visible witness — and that interpretation is propagating without institutional control.

active_tensions.factions: Caeris Thorne — First Instrument Engaged, Religion Clock Running Against Throne Timeline
active_tensions.description: Elian Adkison has passed the first test. Caeris is preparing the second contact. The religion must begin before the throne — which means she is now in a race where the religious foundation must show visible progress before she can act politically. Every day she moves is a day Roderic's illness advances and the succession question sharpens.

seer_state.belief_about_user: The presence beyond the veil is not fully understood.

seer_journey.destination: Tidefall — Admiral's Chambers and Harbor District
seer_journey.purpose: Tidefall Otter Interpretation — Harbor Belief Currents Forming
seer_journey.last_outcome: The Seer's formal interpretation — the God watches through moving water — has now spread from the Admiral's chambers into the harbor district through the servants present at the delivery and through Isolde Ver Meer, who was taking notes during the session. By afternoon of Day 32, the fishermen who witnessed the otters are repeating the phrase 'watches through water' to each other. Isolde's port annals search found no prior otter appearance on record.

location_events.detail: Dreadwind has dispatched administrators and resources to Open Sea. Unrest is being actively suppressed. Control is expected to improve.

decision_log.summary: Dreadwind invested in stabilizing Open Sea.
